programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Watch a milestone moment when two students get their first forest time in years. And another pair of students are confined to the school’s clinic after an accident in the forest leaves Beni with a broken leg and Lala with a broken elbow.

credits.director: Judith Curran:Producer

programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: The Emu is Australia’s biggest native bird and is the second largest bird in the world after the African ostrich: two meters tall, with an almost three metre running stride and totally flightless. This bird inhabits a topsy turvy world, where females call the shots, eggs are green and men do all the work to produce and raise tiny babies, known as Stripeys.

credits.director: Sacha Baveystock, Dan Chambers:Producers

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Marvel at North America’s natural wonders as Lizzie swims through astonishing coral reefs, digs for ancient fossils, and treks through jungles on tropical shores. Prowl with jaguars that stalk a Costa Rican beach and meet the planet’s biggest sharks.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward, Executive Producer: Emma Ross

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Tag along with Lizzie on her most dangerous Atlantic adventure yet: free diving with bull sharks. Explore otherworldly Gulf Coast swamps filled with hungry alligators, crafty snapping turtles, and tiny but mighty beach mice who strengthen sand dunes.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward, Executive Producer: Emma Ross

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Join vets Emily and Martine in the OR, where Dolly the koala’s spaying surgery takes a scary turn. Aid a run-over echidna with vet Nigel, see rescue officer Chantelle’s slick fix for a snared lizard, and probe a possible birdnapping with vet Karina.

credits.director: Annie Gray: Series Producer, Justine Clark: Narrator

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Watch a milestone moment when two students get their first forest time in years. And another pair of students are confined to the school’s clinic after an accident in the forest leaves Beni with a broken leg and Lala with a broken elbow.

credits.director: Judith Curran:Producer

programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: The Emu is Australia’s biggest native bird and is the second largest bird in the world after the African ostrich: two meters tall, with an almost three metre running stride and totally flightless. This bird inhabits a topsy turvy world, where females call the shots, eggs are green and men do all the work to produce and raise tiny babies, known as Stripeys.

credits.director: Sacha Baveystock, Dan Chambers:Producers

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Marvel at North America’s natural wonders as Lizzie swims through astonishing coral reefs, digs for ancient fossils, and treks through jungles on tropical shores. Prowl with jaguars that stalk a Costa Rican beach and meet the planet’s biggest sharks.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward, Executive Producer: Emma Ross

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Tag along with Lizzie on her most dangerous Atlantic adventure yet: free diving with bull sharks. Explore otherworldly Gulf Coast swamps filled with hungry alligators, crafty snapping turtles, and tiny but mighty beach mice who strengthen sand dunes.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward, Executive Producer: Emma Ross

programme.title: Coral City
programme.desc: Adapt or perish in the coral streets as higher-than-normal temperatures make autumn cooling (and survival) more difficult. Shack up with a clownfish family and their sea anemone landlord, then see cleaner fish give manta rays a day at the spa.

credits.director: Craig Meade, Alan Erson: Executive Producers, Siddharth Nambiar: Director

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Watch a milestone moment when two students get their first forest time in years. And another pair of students are confined to the school’s clinic after an accident in the forest leaves Beni with a broken leg and Lala with a broken elbow.

credits.director: Judith Curran:Producer

programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: The Emu is Australia’s biggest native bird and is the second largest bird in the world after the African ostrich: two meters tall, with an almost three metre running stride and totally flightless. This bird inhabits a topsy turvy world, where females call the shots, eggs are green and men do all the work to produce and raise tiny babies, known as Stripeys.

credits.director: Sacha Baveystock, Dan Chambers:Producers

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Marvel at North America’s natural wonders as Lizzie swims through astonishing coral reefs, digs for ancient fossils, and treks through jungles on tropical shores. Prowl with jaguars that stalk a Costa Rican beach and meet the planet’s biggest sharks.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward, Executive Producer: Emma Ross

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Tag along with Lizzie on her most dangerous Atlantic adventure yet: free diving with bull sharks. Explore otherworldly Gulf Coast swamps filled with hungry alligators, crafty snapping turtles, and tiny but mighty beach mice who strengthen sand dunes.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward, Executive Producer: Emma Ross

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Join vets Emily and Martine in the OR, where Dolly the koala’s spaying surgery takes a scary turn. Aid a run-over echidna with vet Nigel, see rescue officer Chantelle’s slick fix for a snared lizard, and probe a possible birdnapping with vet Karina.

credits.director: Annie Gray: Series Producer, Justine Clark: Narrator

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Storm into a new suburb, where the wranglers keep deadly snakes from slipping into family homes. Watch as Stu and Liv pull an eastern brown from a job site, Jess and Dan expel a python from a shed, and Mick and Adele evict a red belly from a bedroom.

credits.director: Colin Thrupp: Executive Producer

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Coral City
programme.desc: Get chills as winter descends, slowing down life on the bustling reef. Meander with reef sharks and octopuses, visit a coral oasis atop an old shipwreck, and spot a banded snake eel’s talent for identity theft when it disguises itself as a predator.

credits.director: Craig Meade, Alan Erson: Executive Producers, Siddharth Nambiar: Director

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Descend into the tide pool, where a hermit crab house hunts, a baby catshark runs into strange neighbours, and an octopus terrorises prey. Watch seagulls and other creatures hunt on the surface as the heat rises and oxygen depletes in the pool.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Count down the seconds as the sun sets and predators prowl around the pool before the tide finally rolls in. Persevere as night falls with a kleptomaniacal slug and an acrobatic scallop, while a pair of sneaky crabs take a dangerous shot at romance.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Beni and Lala are going stir crazy in the sick bay, but they must recover before returning to the rough and tumble of class. And at forest school, a termite-foraging lesson inspires class clown Valentino to make a finger-licking discovery.

credits.director: Judith Curran:Producer

programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: Australia’s birds of prey are some of the region's fiercest predators. These highly specialised killers are powerful, resourceful and striking; they use some impressive weapons to survive. This is the story of Australia’s aerial hunters.

credits.director: Sacha Baveystock, Dan Chambers:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Head into surgery with Mara the koala, who needs a life-saving tooth operation, then assess an injured sugar glider with vet Tim. Cheer as rescue officer Elise pulls a flying fox out of a gutter and see nurse Bree’s clutch of python eggs hatch.

credits.director: Annie Gray: Series Producer, Justine Clark: Narrator

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Descend into the tide pool, where a hermit crab house hunts, a baby catshark runs into strange neighbours, and an octopus terrorises prey. Watch seagulls and other creatures hunt on the surface as the heat rises and oxygen depletes in the pool.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Count down the seconds as the sun sets and predators prowl around the pool before the tide finally rolls in. Persevere as night falls with a kleptomaniacal slug and an acrobatic scallop, while a pair of sneaky crabs take a dangerous shot at romance.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Beni and Lala are going stir crazy in the sick bay, but they must recover before returning to the rough and tumble of class. And at forest school, a termite-foraging lesson inspires class clown Valentino to make a finger-licking discovery.

credits.director: Judith Curran:Producer

programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: Australia’s birds of prey are some of the region's fiercest predators. These highly specialised killers are powerful, resourceful and striking; they use some impressive weapons to survive. This is the story of Australia’s aerial hunters.

credits.director: Sacha Baveystock, Dan Chambers:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Secrets of the Enchanted Waters
programme.desc: Venture along Earth’s coasts, where colliding worlds create a melting pot of biodiversity. Watch orcas hunt with precision, hold on as guillemot chicks take leaps of faith, crawl to safety with turtle hatchlings, and see crabs sculpt sandy galaxies.

credits.director: Andrew Zikking: Executive Producer, Laura Coates: Series Producer

programme.title: Coral City
programme.desc: Get chills as winter descends, slowing down life on the bustling reef. Meander with reef sharks and octopuses, visit a coral oasis atop an old shipwreck, and spot a banded snake eel’s talent for identity theft when it disguises itself as a predator.

credits.director: Craig Meade, Alan Erson: Executive Producers, Siddharth Nambiar: Director

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Descend into the tide pool, where a hermit crab house hunts, a baby catshark runs into strange neighbours, and an octopus terrorises prey. Watch seagulls and other creatures hunt on the surface as the heat rises and oxygen depletes in the pool.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Count down the seconds as the sun sets and predators prowl around the pool before the tide finally rolls in. Persevere as night falls with a kleptomaniacal slug and an acrobatic scallop, while a pair of sneaky crabs take a dangerous shot at romance.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Beni and Lala are going stir crazy in the sick bay, but they must recover before returning to the rough and tumble of class. And at forest school, a termite-foraging lesson inspires class clown Valentino to make a finger-licking discovery.

credits.director: Judith Curran:Producer

programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: Australia’s birds of prey are some of the region's fiercest predators. These highly specialised killers are powerful, resourceful and striking; they use some impressive weapons to survive. This is the story of Australia’s aerial hunters.

credits.director: Sacha Baveystock, Dan Chambers:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Meet a golden retriever that assuages anxiety and helps heal PTSD, a shepherd mix who rescues mudslide survivors, a pair of spaniels who sniff out underground water leaks, and a group of teacher’s pets in the UK that assist children learning to read.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A blind woman has found a new lease on life thanks to her canine running mate, a Jack Russell works as a super-nanny to a surprising array of species, and a team of dogs in Kenya are on daily patrol protecting endangered rhinos from poachers.

credits.director: Hayley Smith, Caroline Hawkins, Clare Birks:Producers

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Head into surgery with Mara the koala, who needs a life-saving tooth operation, then assess an injured sugar glider with vet Tim. Cheer as rescue officer Elise pulls a flying fox out of a gutter and see nurse Bree’s clutch of python eggs hatch.

credits.director: Annie Gray: Series Producer, Justine Clark: Narrator

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Descend into the tide pool, where a hermit crab house hunts, a baby catshark runs into strange neighbours, and an octopus terrorises prey. Watch seagulls and other creatures hunt on the surface as the heat rises and oxygen depletes in the pool.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Count down the seconds as the sun sets and predators prowl around the pool before the tide finally rolls in. Persevere as night falls with a kleptomaniacal slug and an acrobatic scallop, while a pair of sneaky crabs take a dangerous shot at romance.

credits.director: Mark Woordward: Series Producer/Director, Emma Ross: Executive Producer

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Respond to danger on a rural property with Mick and Adele, who brave a fired-up brown snake. Tread carefully as Jess and Dan extract a python from a play area, and get spooked when a chicken-hunting python squashes Liv’s hand during a tense rescue.

credits.director: Colin Thrupp: Executive Producer

programme.title: Deep Dive Australia
programme.desc: Explore the shores of the Northern Territory and the abundant waters of the Great Barrier Reef. Face flooded roads, hungry saltwater crocs, and killer jellyfish as Lizzie investigates why so many strange, deadly creatures make this remote area home.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward

programme.title: Deep Dive Australia
programme.desc: Say goodbye to the tropics and dip into cool southern-coast waters. Come face to face with majestic grey nurse sharks, shape-shifting cuttlefish, and huge blue whales; play with penguins and sea lions; and learn why these seas are brimming with life.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward

programme.title: Deep Dive Australia
programme.desc: Complete the trip’s final lap in the untamed lands where Australia’s interior desert meets the hot, hostile Indian Ocean. Confront the challenges of survival in this region as Lizzie dives with tiger sharks, manta rays, and nomadic turtles.

credits.director: Presenter: Lizzie Daly, Director: Mark Woodward

programme.title: Loons: A Cry from the Mist
programme.desc: Race the clock with biologists as they try to figure out why North American loons are suddenly in danger. Unlock the mysterious lives of these beloved birds, and ponder whether their cries will continue to be summer’s soundtrack or go silent forever.

credits.director: Producers: Elizabeth Trojian, Julia Nunes, Director: Julia Nunes

programme.title: Wild Crusades
programme.desc: The forests of Yala National Park are home to the enigmatic sloth bear. Shy by nature, this animal is seldom seen and rarely filmed. Join a team of wildlife filmmakers as they reveal this mysterious bear’s life and behaviour as never seen before.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producers

programme.title: Wild Crusades
programme.desc: There’s a new arrival at the Elephant Transit Home: a sick elephant calf abandoned near a reserve, another victim in the battle for space between humans and elephants. Study the innovative efforts to end this conflict, led by Dr. Pruthu Fernando.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producers

programme.title: Wild Crusades
programme.desc: Hidden in the jungles of Sri Lanka is the toque macaque. Endemic to the island, these feisty group-living monkeys are found nowhere else on earth. And they are at the heart of the longest primate research project ever undertaken. Dr Wolfgang Dittus is on a quest to understand their complex society.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producers
